comments.author: RebarBotNava
comments.body: Re-parented from the Janitor Cleanup 2026-07-07 epic into the Jira reconciler correctness epic (adept-hedge-stain) — this is reconciler work (risk #3), not generic cleanup.

comments.author: RebarBotNava
comments.body: Root cause CONFIRMED on current main: route_inbound_probe (reconcile.py:317-330) builds the inbound-delete Mutation with payload {'reason':'hard_delete', ...} but _apply_inbound_delete (apply_inbound.py:184) reads payload.get('probe_outcome', 'out_of_window') -> the hard_delete branch is UNREACHABLE (always defaults to out_of_window); the create_after_hard_delete follow_on (apply_inbound.py:202-208) is built but the applier consumer (applier.py:467-469) only handles kind=='suppress_pair', so it's dropped. Fix plan: (1) leaf branch = payload.get('reason') or payload.get('probe_outcome','out_of_window'); update fixtures test_inbound_leaf_bodies.py (redirect + hard_delete). (2) applier consumer: on follow_on action=='create_after_hard_delete', look up the still-present LOCAL ticket by local_id, reconstruct create fields via outbound_fields._map_local_to_jira_fields, build a standard outbound CREATE Mutation, and add it to the pass's outbound mutation list so it flows through create_one (JQL dedup + bind_pending/bind_confirm) — no mid-loop create_one, no typed-dispatch bypass. (3) binding_store: on rebind, drop stale reverse[old_key] in the same save. (4) idempotency: JQL dedup prevents double-create; REST-budget exhaustion defers to next pass. (5) guard so only genuine hard_delete re-creates; repoint TODO(epic-3e36) at apply_inbound.py; clear .joe-janitor/report flag. Not yet implemented.

comments.author: RebarBotNava
comments.body: HANDOFF: set back to open (no code written; root-cause + fix plan are in the earlier comment). plan-review attestation present but may be stale vs current main — re-run rebar review-plan before claiming if the claim gate blocks.

comments.author: RebarBotNava
comments.body: Implemented + pushed as Gerrit change 432 (stacked on A3/424). All 8 ACs covered by tests in test_hard_delete_recreate.py + test_binding_store.py; full make test green locally; LLM-Review pending. Will close after Verified +1.

compiled_state.description:
# Finish Jira hard-delete -> outbound re-create (replaces lost epic-3e36)

## Why
On a Jira hard-delete the reconciler preserves local content (ADR 0027/0028) BUT never re-creates the Jira
issue. The path is broken at two joints:
- **Key mismatch**: `route_inbound_probe` (reconcile.py:316-324, ARCHIVED_OR_MOVED branch) builds the
  inbound-delete Mutation with `payload={"reason": "hard_delete", ...}`, but `_apply_inbound_delete`
  (apply_inbound.py:184) reads `payload.get("probe_outcome", "out_of_window")` — so the hard_delete branch is
  UNREACHABLE (always defaults to out_of_window).
- **Dropped follow-on**: even when the branch is reached it builds a `follow_on`
  (apply_inbound.py:202-210, keys `direction/action/target/local_id`, `action="create_after_hard_delete"`, NO
  `fields`), but the applier consumer (applier.py:467-468) only handles `follow_on.get("kind")=="suppress_pair"`
  — the `create_after_hard_delete` follow-on (which sets `action`, not `kind`) is silently ignored. This is the
  `epic-3e36` TODO (apply_inbound.py:209-210).

## What (one coherent path: inbound detect -> standard outbound CREATE mutation)
1. **Canonical key = `reason`.** Change `_apply_inbound_delete` (apply_inbound.py:184) to read
   `payload.get("reason") or payload.get("probe_outcome", "out_of_window")` (accept `reason`, keep back-compat).
   Update ALL `probe_outcome` fixture sites in `tests/unit/rebar_reconciler/mutate/test_inbound_leaf_bodies.py`
   — verified there are exactly TWO: line 281 (`payload = {"probe_outcome": outcome}`, the parametrized test
   covering all four outcomes incl. hard_delete) and line 332 (`payload={"probe_outcome": "redirect", ...}`).
   Both must carry `reason` (parametrize both keys, or switch to `reason`). (The review's guess of line 289 is
   stale — no `probe_outcome` there on current main.)
2. **Reconstruct `fields`.** The follow-on carries `local_id` but not `fields`, and `create_one`
   (dispatch_one.py:147-156, 168) REQUIRES a `mutation["fields"]`. Fetch the still-present LOCAL ticket by
   `local_id` and map it via `_map_local_to_jira_fields(ticket, binding_store=binding_store,
   local_ticket_types=local_ticket_types, emit_detach_clear=False)` (outbound_fields.py:112-117) — the SAME mapper
   the outbound differ uses (returns summary/description/issuetype/priority/status/assignee, and `parent` only when
   the parent is bound). Pass the SAME `local_ticket_types` map the outbound path builds (local_id -> ticket_type,
   from the loaded local tickets) so the non-epic-parent suppression guard behaves identically to a normal
   outbound create; if it is not readily in scope, build a minimal one from the single fetched ticket (its own
   type) — an unbound/absent parent is simply omitted and reconciled on a later pass (create_one is
   parent-tolerant).
   - **Lookup mechanism (explicit):** the applier inbound loop (applier.py:455-474) has NO `local_tickets` in
     scope, and there is no by-id helper. `repo_root` IS in scope; read `<repo_root>/.tickets-tracker/<local_id>/
     .cache.json` `state` directly (single-ticket, cheap), falling back to `reconcile_check.load_local_tickets`
     only if the cache is absent.
   - **Failure path:** wrap the `.cache.json` read in `try/except (json.JSONDecodeError, OSError)`. If the local
     ticket is absent, the cache is malformed, or `_map_local_to_jira_fields` yields empty/no-summary, do NOT emit
     a create — log + skip (a hard-deleted ticket with no local content is not re-creatable). Tested.
3. **Wiring (dedicated pre-batch side-channel — must flow THROUGH `_apply_batch`).** Introduce a new
   `pending_hard_delete_creates: list[dict]` local in `apply()` (declared next to `pending_bug_tickets` at
   applier.py:453). In the inbound loop (L455-474), when `follow_on.get("action")=="create_after_hard_delete"`,
   build a batch-dict CREATE entry (a **dict**, NOT a typed `Mutation`, because `outbound_list` is already a
   `list[dict]`) shaped like a normal outbound create — `{"action":"create","key":<jira-target-or-"">,
   "local_id":local_id,"fields":<mapped fields>}` (match the dict keys `_apply_batch`/`create_one` read: `fields`
   + `local_id`; use `_mutation_to_batch_dict` if you build a Mutation first) — and append it to
   `pending_hard_delete_creates`.
   Then, AFTER `outbound_list` is materialised + suppression-filtered (applier.py:486-490) and BEFORE the
   `_apply_batch(outbound_list, …)` dispatch (L495), do `outbound_list.extend(pending_hard_delete_creates)`.
   **This is DELIBERATELY unlike `pending_bug_tickets`**, which drains in the `finally` AFTER `_apply_batch`
   (L503-521) via a separate `in_process_cli` call and thus BYPASSES `create_one`. The re-create MUST go through
   `_apply_batch` -> `create_one` so it gets JQL dedup + `bind_confirm`. It shares only the *collection* idea
   (a side-channel list populated in the inbound loop), not the drain site. No mid-loop `create_one`, no
   typed-dispatch bypass.

### Follow-on dict schema (both discriminants coexist)
The applier's inbound-loop follow-on routing keys off TWO independent discriminants, kept as-is:
- `follow_on["kind"] == "suppress_pair"` -> `suppression.record(...)` (existing).
- `follow_on["action"] == "create_after_hard_delete"` -> the new re-create path (this ticket). Keys:
  `direction, action, target, local_id` (no `fields` — reconstructed in step 2).
A given follow-on carries exactly one of `kind`/`action`; the routing checks both independently.
4. **Idempotency + deferral.** `create_one`'s JQL dedup (`labels = "rebar-id:<local_id>"`, dispatch_one.py:
   194-219) prevents double-create on retry. If `rest_calls >= _REST_CALL_BUDGET` (=200, dispatch_one.py:97,
   188-192) `create_one` issues zero REST calls, appends to `deferred_creates`, returns None -> the re-create
   DEFERS to the next pass (same as the normal create path). Test BOTH partial-failure directions:
   (a) create ok but a following rebind/confirm fails; (b) create deferred by budget exhaustion.
5. **Stale reverse-index cleanup.** `bind_confirm(local_id, jira_key)` (binding_store.py:276-288) overwrites
   `bindings[local_id]["jira_key"]` and sets `reverse[jira_key]=local_id` but never removes the OLD key's reverse
   entry (no `rebind` method exists; only `unbind` pops reverse). Fix: at the TOP of `bind_confirm`, read
   `old_key = entry.get("jira_key")` BEFORE overwriting; if `old_key and old_key != jira_key`, `reverse.pop(old_key,
   None)` in the SAME save. Edits `binding_store.py`.
6. **Guard + housekeeping.** Guard the re-create so ONLY a genuine `reason=="hard_delete"` triggers it (redirect/
   out_of_window/trash never re-create). Repoint/remove the `TODO(epic-3e36)` at apply_inbound.py:209-210. Update
   ADR 0027/0028 to document the now-completed re-create behavior (inbound consumer injects an outbound CREATE
   into the same pass). Clear the `.joe-janitor/report` flag if present.

## Scope (files EDITED)
- `src/rebar/_engine/rebar_reconciler/apply_inbound.py` — key fix (read `reason`) + TODO repoint
- `src/rebar/_engine/rebar_reconciler/applier.py` — consume `create_after_hard_delete`; NEW import
  `from .outbound_fields import _map_local_to_jira_fields` (the new cross-module coupling lives HERE — applier ->
  outbound_fields); local-ticket lookup; `pending_hard_delete_creates` injection into `outbound_list`
- `src/rebar/_engine/rebar_reconciler/binding_store.py` — stale `reverse[old_key]` cleanup in `bind_confirm`
- `tests/unit/rebar_reconciler/mutate/test_inbound_leaf_bodies.py` — fixture key updates (L281, L332)
- new test file `tests/unit/rebar_reconciler/test_hard_delete_recreate.py` — consumer + rebind + failure + guard
- `docs/adr/0027*.md` / `0028*.md` — document completed behavior

**Read-only (NOT edited), noted for the coupling:** `outbound_fields.py` (`_map_local_to_jira_fields` is
imported/called by applier.py, not modified); `dispatch_one.py` (`create_one` behavior relied on, not changed).

## Acceptance Criteria
Each AC names its proving test (all under `tests/unit/rebar_reconciler/`; run with
`pytest tests/unit/rebar_reconciler/ -q`).
- [ ] `_apply_inbound_delete` reads `reason` (canonical) with `probe_outcome` fallback; the hard_delete branch is
      reachable from EITHER key. Both fixture sites (test_inbound_leaf_bodies.py L281, L332) updated. Proof:
      `test_hard_delete_recreate.py::test_reason_key_enters_hard_delete_branch` AND
      `::test_probe_outcome_key_still_enters_hard_delete_branch` (both keys asserted; the fallback is NOT dead).
- [ ] The applier consumes `action=="create_after_hard_delete"`: fetches the local ticket by `local_id`,
      reconstructs `fields` via `_map_local_to_jira_fields`, and injects a batch-dict CREATE into `outbound_list`
      before `_apply_batch`. Proof: `test_hard_delete_recreate.py::test_recreate_injected_into_outbound_batch`
      asserts the fake acli client records a `create_issue` for the local_id (i.e. it flowed through `create_one`,
      not a post-batch bypass).
- [ ] Consumer failure path: local ticket absent / `.cache.json` malformed / no mappable summary -> log + skip,
      no create emitted. Proof: `::test_recreate_skips_when_local_ticket_absent` and
      `::test_recreate_skips_on_malformed_cache`.
- [ ] Idempotency + deferral: JQL dedup prevents a double-create on retry; REST-budget exhaustion defers to the
      next pass. Proof: `::test_recreate_jql_dedup_no_double_create` (create-ok, second pass dedups) and
      `::test_recreate_deferred_when_budget_exhausted`.
- [ ] `bind_confirm` deletes the stale `reverse[old_key]` (read BEFORE overwrite) in the same save. Proof:
      `test_binding_store.py::test_bind_confirm_rebind_drops_stale_reverse_key` asserts `reverse` has no dangling
      old-key entry after a rebind.
- [ ] Guard: only `reason=="hard_delete"` re-creates (redirect/out_of_window/trash do not). Proof:
      `::test_non_hard_delete_branches_do_not_recreate` (parametrized over the other outcomes).
- [ ] Housekeeping (each with a concrete check):
      (a) `grep -rn "epic-3e36" src/rebar/_engine/rebar_reconciler/apply_inbound.py` returns NO stale TODO (either
      removed, or repointed to `c244`);
      (b) `grep -rl "re-create" docs/adr/0027*.md docs/adr/0028*.md` (or the equivalent updated-behavior phrase)
      matches — the ADR now documents that a hard-delete re-creates the Jira issue via a same-pass outbound CREATE;
      (c) `test -e .joe-janitor/report && grep -L "hard-delete" .joe-janitor/report` — the stale janitor flag for
      this item is cleared (or the file is absent).
- [ ] End-to-end test: a single reconcile pass driven from an inbound hard-delete probe result produces an
      outbound CREATE for the still-present local ticket, asserted via the fake acli client's recorded create
      call. Proof: `::test_hard_delete_recreate_end_to_end_one_pass` — distinct from the unit branch/consumer
      tests (it drives the full `apply()` pass, not `_apply_inbound_delete` in isolation).
compiled_state.title: Finish Jira hard-delete -> outbound re-create (replaces lost epic-3e36)

verify_commands.dd_text: inbound hard-delete -> outbound re-create in one pass; no double-create on retry
